title: Fixed-PVM Born Rule Uniqueness from Fisher Non-Expansion and Operational Calibration
abstract: Fix a finite dimension $d \geq 2$ and a fixed rank-1 PVM $M=\{|e_1\rangle\langle e_1|,\ldots,|e_d\rangle\langle e_d|\}$ on ${\bf C}^d$. Let $P_M:\mathbb{CP}^{d-1}\toΔ^{d-1}$ be a readout map on pure states. We prove that three primitives force the Born rule for this fixed measurement: (i) square-root regularity of $R_M=\sqrt{P_M}$ along Fubini-Study geodesics, (ii) the universal readout Cramer-Rao bound $F_{\rm cl}\leq F_Q$ on smooth pure-state curves, and (iii) operational calibration on basis preparations $P_M([e_i])=δ_i$. The geometric core is a rigidity theorem for Fisher-non-expanding self-maps of the probability simplex: after conjugation by the square-root chart, such maps become round-metric 1-Lipschitz self-maps of the positive spherical orthant, and vertex fixing forces the identity. The main readout theorem is dimensionwise, fixed-PVM, and pure-state only. Escort-class Born uniqueness and the Markov/coarse-graining routes appear as corollaries or alternative routes.
summary: This paper proves that the Born rule is the unique readout map for a fixed measurement if one assumes the map is regular, obeys a quantum Cramer-Rao bound, and is correctly calibrated. It achieves this by showing that such constraints force the readout map to be a rigid, identity-preserving map in a geometric space.
main_problem: Determining whether a specific set of geometric and operational primitives is sufficient to uniquely force the Born rule for a fixed rank-1 projective measurement.
main_result: The paper proves that if a readout map satisfies square-root regularity, a universal readout Cramer-Rao bound (Fisher non-expansion), and operational calibration, then the map must be the Born rule.
method: The authors use an information-geometric approach, employing a square-root chart to transform the readout map into a 1-Lipschitz self-map of a spherical orthant, then apply geometric rigidity theorems.
model_or_system: A finite-dimensional complex Hilbert space (dimension d >= 2) and a fixed rank-1 projective valued measurement (PVM) on pure states.
key_observables: Classical Fisher Information (Fcl), Quantum Fisher Information (FQ), and the readout probabilities of the PVM.
important_parameters: Dimension d, Fubini-Study metric, and Fisher-Rao/Hellinger metric.
main_assumptions: The result is restricted to pure states and a fixed measurement; it assumes the universal readout Cramer-Rao bound as an admissibility postulate.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of Born rule uniqueness, defines the geometric framework using square-root charts, presents the Vertex Rigidity Lemma, proves the main readout theorem via Fisher non-expansion, and discusses corollaries like escort-class uniqueness.
why_interesting: This provides a new, purely geometric perspective on the foundations of quantum mechanics, showing how information-theoretic constraints (Cramer-Rao bounds) can recover the standard Born rule without needing Gleason-type basis-independence.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
